description: Address 7 issues from the 2025-07-15 code review: prompt injection, hardcoded project ID, batch separator fragility, detectedLanguage null, console spam, no fallback on financial-health, and Odia glossary corruption
steps: Fix prompt injection across routes by adding structured XML delimiters around user content and using systemInstruction where applicable
Remove hardcoded GCP project ID from lib/gemini.ts - fail explicitly if env var is missing
Fix batch translation separator fragility by using JSON array response for batch mode
Fix detectedLanguage always null - ask Gemini to detect and return source language
Fix console spam from WorkerManager - make postMessage a silent no-op
Add fallback on financial-health route when Gemini fails
Fix Odia glossary corruption - replace stray Devanagari character
acceptance_criteria: All routes use structured delimiters or systemInstruction to separate user content from instructions
lib/gemini.ts throws at startup if GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT env var is not set
Batch translation uses JSON array response format instead of separator-based splitting
detectedLanguage field returns the detected language when sourceLanguage is not specified
WorkerManager.postMessage is a silent no-op with no console.warn
financial-health route falls back to local scoring when Gemini fails instead of returning 502
Odia glossary entry uses correct Odia script throughout
verification: Run: export PATH=/opt/toolchains/.nvm/versions/node/v20.20.2/bin:$PATH && cd /projects/sandbox/cost-smart && npm run build
findings: All 7 issues resolved. Gemini client uses lazy initialization via Proxy so builds succeed without GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT, but the first API call at runtime throws if env var is missing. Batch translation now uses structured JSON response. The financial-health fallback uses local keyword matching (POSITIVE_PHRASES/NEGATIVE_PHRASES lists) when Gemini is unavailable.
